$comment: File-level open/proprietary partition for the open-core SDK boundary lint.
The lint rule (no-open-to-proprietary in .dependency-cruiser.cjs) forbids any
OPEN source file from importing (value OR type) a NON-OPEN file.
NON-OPEN = every src file that is not in the OPEN set below (proprietary dirs,
wrapper surfaces, the proprietary files inside otherwise-open split dirs, and
Patterns are JS-regex strings matched against the repo-relative module path
exceptions out of an otherwise-open directory.
openRootFilesNote: src/version.ts is a trivial version-string leaf (imports only node builtins +
package.json, no internal src deps) consumed by open files (transcript/writer,
mcp/client); it is open-core. src/main.ts is the wrapper entrypoint → NON-OPEN.
proprietaryFilesNote: Documented for readers — already excluded from OPEN by the negative lookaheads
/ openFilesInProprietaryDirs carve-outs above, so they fall into NON-OPEN
src/tool/registry.ts — the assembleToolPool tool-pool ASSEMBLER (a wrapper
surface: the harness assembles its full pool incl. feature tools; the open
SDK provides tool/buildTool + the generic tool primitives). No OPEN file
FEATURE tools (learning / review / workflows). Generic tools (FileRead,
Bash, Grep, Task*, Web*, Skill*, Memory, …) stay OPEN.
src/commands/* EXCEPT src/commands/types.ts — the slash-command IMPLEMENTATIONS
are a surface feature; types.ts (the command-type CONTRACT) is kept OPEN
because the open slash loader skills/commands.ts depends on PromptCommand.
proprietaryDirsNote: Fully NON-OPEN (proprietary or wrapper) — not enumerated here because NON-OPEN
is the complement of OPEN. For readers:
proprietary: learning, learning-layer, server, workflows, daemon, cron,
channels, plugins, tasks, agent, review, router(rest), agent/sessionDb,
wrapper (proprietary for this lint): cli, openai, mission, ui, main.ts.
